meaning: adj.沉没的,凹陷的 vbl.sink的过去分词
examples: Her eyes were sunken and black-ringed.
The sunken sail boat was a glimmer of white on the bottom.
This sighting occurred during my dive to a sunken wreck off Sardinia.
She had lost weight: her eyes were sunken and her pale forearms looked like Popsicle sticks.
Out back, a brick wrap-around patio leads to a sunken pool surrounded by mature foliage.
